title: campanula rotundifolia subsp. rhomboidalis
intro: Campanula rotundifolia subsp. rhomboidalis, also known as the Scottish bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family that is native to the mountainous regions of Scotland. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, antiviral, and antifungal properties, which can help to boost the immune system, protect against cell damage, and support overall well-being. In herbal preparations, Campanula rotundifolia subsp. rhomboidalis is often used as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is also used in traditional folk remedies to treat a range of health conditions.
